title: Introduction to ICT for Development

prerequisites.text: Admission into the Master's degree specialising in Computer Science, or permission from the course convener.

outline: The goal is to understand basic ideas underlying ICT4D and how they are used in practice. Course content includes: Introduction to key terminology around socio-economic development; Key concepts in ICT4D (e.g. social inclusion, after access); Case studies in specific domains including healthcare, agriculture, mobile money, education, etc.; Critical evaluation of ICT4D projects.
assessment: Practical assignments: 80%; Case Study Presentation: 10%; Class Participation: 10%.
